title: Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary lies on the east side of the Uhl River in Mandi District of Himachal Pradesh.
overview.description: Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary lies on the east side of the Uhl River in Mandi District of Himachal Pradesh. It was notified in 1999 and covers an area of 132.37 km2 (51.11 sq mi). The slopes of the sanctuary are covered with Alpine forest. It is home to various species of animals and birds.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Barot, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Barot, a village originally developed in the 1920s for Shanan Hydro Project, is now a tourist location in Mandi district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Barot was difficult to access until 1975, when a road was opened.

topPlaces.name: Jogindernagar
topPlaces.description: Jogindernagar, or Jogindar Nagar ([dʒoːˈɡɪnd̪ər nəˈɡər]), is a municipality, and a sub district in Mandi district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Named after Raja Joginder Sen, the hill station is the terminus of the 163-kilometre-long (101 mi) Kangra Valley narrow-gauge railway.

topPlaces.name: Joginder Nagar Valley
topPlaces.description: Joginder Nagar Valley also known as Bhangaal Valley or Bhangaal region is a valley in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. The valley contains the town of Joginder Nagar, Bir-Billing, Chauntra and various other hill resorts surrounded by mountains on all sides.

items.note: Barot, a village originally developed in the 1920s for Shanan Hydro Project, is now a tourist location in Mandi district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Barot was difficult to access until 1975, when a road was opened.

Jogindernagar, or Jogindar Nagar ([dʒoːˈɡɪnd̪ər nəˈɡər]), is a municipality, and a sub district in Mandi district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Named after Raja Joginder Sen, the hill station is the terminus of the 163-kilometre-long (101 mi) Kangra Valley narrow-gauge railway.

Joginder Nagar Valley also known as Bhangaal Valley or Bhangaal region is a valley in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. The valley contains the town of Joginder Nagar, Bir-Billing, Chauntra and various other hill resorts surrounded by mountains on all sides.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (32 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (148 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nargu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Barot, Himachal Pradesh, Jogindernagar, Joginder Nagar Valley.
